### Post by Shlomir on 2008-12-08
Hi there,

I"ve upgraded to 8.10 and can't get out of 640x480 res, even though my Nvidia drivers installed OK.. Here is my xorg:

```
# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# Note that some configuration settings that could be done previously
# in this file, now are automatically configured by the server and settings
# here are ignored.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Configured Video Device"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Configured Monitor"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Monitor		"Configured Monitor"
	Device		"Configured Video Device"
EndSection
```

What line do i need to add to get the hi-res settings?

Thanks

---

### Post by DaveyG on 2008-12-08
Are you %100 sure the Drivers are enabled/installed for your Graphics Card?

Try checking under System -> Administration -> Hardware Drivers, and enable the Driver (If any is listed)

If the above fails, try installing nvidia-glx-177 from Synaptic Package Manager.

You might want to reconigure XServer too: ```
sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
```

---

### Post by Shlomir on 2008-12-08
Yeah that did diddly-squat. The Nvida 172 driver is active, now it goes to 640x480..this is a 32 bit fx5700 card, I could do better res than this !

---

### Post by poopteam on 2008-12-08
On [http://propellerheadadmin.com/tutorials/ubuntu/7-ubuntu-810-nvidia-x-server-settings-doesnt-save-configuration](http://propellerheadadmin.com/tutorials/ubuntu/7-ubuntu-810-nvidia-x-server-settings-doesnt-save-configuration) I found the following...

$sudo nvidia-xconfig

This will create a new xorg.conf file which can be modified by nvidia-settings.  Reboot the computer to use this new file.

$sudo nvidia-settings

Change the settings you wish and it will be saved.  Hope that helps.

pt
